description_long: This street food stall features a weathered wooden roof with overlapping shingles, supported by a sturdy metal frame. The main body of the cart is constructed from metal, adorned with various stickers and signs, and houses two large open trays filled with food. A rusted bicycle is attached to one side, serving as the primary means of mobility, while a large gas cylinder and a blue hand truck are positioned nearby, suggesting the use of gas-powered cooking equipment. The stall is set on a wheeled base, allowing it to be pushed or cycled to different locations. Small triangular flags with a white face logo hang from the roof, adding a touch of branding. The overall appearance is utilitarian and slightly worn, typical of urban street food vendors.
description: A weathered metal and wood street food stall with a shingled roof, attached bicycle, gas cylinder, and open food trays, decorated with stickers and small flags.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the food trays, shingled roof, gas cylinder, blue hand truck, and a partial view of the bicycle.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the attached bicycle, food trays, and a small blue paint can near the wheel.
description_view_2: Rear-left view focusing on the bicycle attachment, food trays, and the back of the stall.
description_view_3: Rear-right view showing the blue hand truck, gas cylinder, and the back side of the food stall with visible stickers.
attribute_signage: Small triangular red flags with a white face logo hang from the roof corners; no other visible text or signage.
attribute_serving_counter: A rectangular metal counter runs along the front of the stall, with open trays for food and a small area for transactions; no glass shield is present.
attribute_food_display: Food is displayed openly in two large metal trays set into the counter, fully visible and easily accessible, with no protective covering.
attribute_architectural_style: traditional wooden cart with metal frame
